description_long: This cowshed is an L-shaped, single-story structure with a prominent dark blue hip roof that covers both wings of the building. The exterior walls are finished in a light gray material, punctuated by several rectangular blue-framed windows distributed along the longer sides. The main entrance features a solid, dark-colored door with a simple design, located at the end of one wing. The roof is steeply pitched, likely to facilitate water runoff, and the overall form is utilitarian, prioritizing function over ornamentation. The windows are evenly spaced, providing natural light and ventilation to the interior, while the lack of decorative elements suggests a focus on practicality. The building's proportions and features are typical of agricultural outbuildings found in rural or peri-urban environments.
description: An L-shaped, single-story cowshed with a dark blue hip roof, light gray walls, and blue-framed rectangular windows. The design is utilitarian with a simple main entrance and evenly spaced windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance door, two windows on the short wing, and several windows along the longer wing under the prominent hip roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long wing with five evenly spaced windows and the intersection of the two roof sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the uninterrupted length of the long wing with five windows and the continuous hip roof.
description_view_3: End view of the short wing, showing two windows and the gable end of the roof, with the long wing extending to the right.
attribute_door_style: single solid door, likely metal or wood, with minimal design
attribute_window_presence: multiple rectangular windows, five on the long side, two on the short wing, blue-framed
